item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Makes no sense. What is any of this about?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Basically, this was document was written shortly after their capture. Dr. Hayward would be let out and restricted to SCP Foundation Sites after a period of time under study.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok so why is SCP-2999-B in containment?

Seams to me they should be working for the foundation…. they have stranger A/B class personal working for them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm guessing you're not joking here, so… this is likely a question for the technical team and should be asked in the main forums rather than the discussion thread of a particular SCP article. But it would likely be an add-on or some other personal setting on your side, rather than a tool that can be implemented from your wikidot profile settings.

If you are joking because the pictures are scary… that's a bunch of text you don't need, I guess :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, how do I disable images on wikidot? Thanks

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, staring at that picture was an educational experience!
I didn't even know what the sensation of your brain trying to crawl out of your ears to escape the utter horror of what the hell you're witnessing felt like before looking at it!
Take my +1. I guess.

item.title: black rabbit?
item.content:encoded: really does remind me of the black rabbit company, are these two related or is it just a coincidence

item.title: Re: update
item.content:encoded:
Whoops, didn't see your comment

The answer is yes

item.title: Re: Thank you for picking up on that.
item.content:encoded: Oh, okay gotcha. Well, I guess only time will tell.

item.title: Re: Thank you for picking up on that.
item.content:encoded:
Sorta. I've only ever told people what I want them to know.

I wanted you to think SCP-2999-A is Clovis up until now, now that NSH is released.

Think about it. Is Clovis really capable of hurting Stuart like this? You read NSH, you know she loves him like family. Why would she do this?

These "clues" are evidence of something else. Clovis is involved, but SCP-2999-A is not Clovis.

item.title: Re: Thank you for picking up on that.
item.content:encoded: As soon as I read your reply I literally paced around my house trying to decipher what you meant by that. Now that I'm writing this, I think I know: you never outright said that SCP-2999-a was Clovis, but I saw clues that led me to believe that she was (sorry that I neglected to mention that in my first post), right?

item.title: Thank you for picking up on that.
item.content:encoded: You caught me in a lie. I never actually said SCP-2999-A was Clovis in any of the stories, did I?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait. After reading "No safe haven" I'm confused. Stuart knew Clovis as a child, so does he not know that scp 2999-a is Clovis or does he just not remember?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Copy that.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Only one I object to is the last one. Site-45 is located in Las Vegas according to Pitch Haven canon, not Australia.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Alright. I have a few to propose for this skip. First, the mention of Prometheus Labs can be turned into a in-line link to the PL Hub. Additionally, since Site-45-C is a common factor among almost all the Pitch Haven skips, this can be turned into a link to the PH Hub. Finally, there is also a dossier for Site-45 on the site, which can be used at another mention of Site-45 in the article. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

SCP-2999 instances are to be stored in separate secure holding cells on Site-45-C. Requests for personal items by SCP-2999-B may be approved by personnel with Level 3/2999 clearance or higher, provided they remain within reason. Requests made by SCP-2999-A require Level 4/2999 clearance to be carried out.

To this:

SCP-2999 instances are to be stored in separate secure holding cells on Site-45-C. Requests for personal items by SCP-2999-B may be approved by personnel with Level 3/2999 clearance or higher, provided they remain within reason. Requests made by SCP-2999-A require Level 4/2999 clearance to be carried out.

Changing this:

SCP-2999 is the collective designation given to two separate entities originating from the first underground floor of the main base of Prometheus Labs, specifically designated SCP-2999-A and SCP-2999-B.

To this:

SCP-2999 is the collective designation given to two separate entities originating from the first underground floor of the main base of Prometheus Labs, specifically designated SCP-2999-A and SCP-2999-B.

Changing this:

These Documents are provided under jurisdiction of Hayward Protocol, and may only be viewed by Site-45 psychiatric staff, staff with specialized 2999 clearance, the current Site-45 Administrator, and those with O5 designations.

To this:

These Documents are provided under jurisdiction of Hayward Protocol, and may only be viewed by Site-45 psychiatric staff, staff with specialized 2999 clearance, the current Site-45 Administrator, and those with O5 designations.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yumenikkkkkki!The icon of Yumenikki.
At the bottom of the second one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cool, thanks!

Maybe eventually I will figure out who "A" is…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes, but 2 things:

These demiurges, The Crafters, made other animal spirits (as well as like actual animals) and taught some of them the magic of creation (The Scholars) but some of them couldn't learn it (The Honorables).

Well, they could learn it, and they did. the Crafters just chose 100 of their best to learn the real shit (which is why they're called scholars), with the intent that they'd teach the honorables, that way, everyone'd be pretty much on the same page (which is why they're called honorables, since they're meant to be equal.)

However, with the war and all, this sorta divided them into a lower and upper class. The Scholars got government support, and the honorables did not.

And second, they did not die of SCP-1903 infection.

E: also, try https://www.mindmeister.com/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Only some of it. There are pieces that I still don't get.

+ show block
– hide block


God was a complete asshat, and created a bunch of demiurges in the Garden of Eden (near Las Vegas) as animal spirits. These demiurges, The Crafters, made other animal spirits (as well as like actual animals) and taught some of them the magic of creation (The Scholars) and a third class that was supposed to learn from the scholars (The Honorables).

Then God made man. The 13th Crafter, SCP-682, corrupted man into eating the fruit of the tree of knowledge. God got mad and punished all the animal spirits by introducing hunger and cast man out of Eden.

The animal spirits split into factions and there was a civil war. Fredrick, Agatha, and Clovis were one faction. Suiward and Sara were another faction. I think there was a third one maybe?

Things got really ugly and there were crucifixions and dismemberments and trees made from arms and other horrible stuff. Clovis was torn apart, which apparently has something to do with her ability to occupy networks now.

Then God finally decides to invent death and wanders off because He is bored and a complete asshole.

Some animal spirits from eden are reincarnated as people. Others seem to be trapped in weird cursed forms. Some become neoplatonic gods and stuff. Over time they do weird magic stuff.

Eventually Suiward and Sara are reincarnated as Stewart and Sarah. They join the Foundation in the 60s and get mixed up in all this weird crap that they are connected with even if they don't remember.

They eventually both die again.

Then Clovis gets involved with Prothemeus Labs and gets into their computers somehow. She manipulates them into resurrecting Stewart as this weird cat thing and she pretends to be Sarah to trick him.

Or something like this. I really need to reread it and update my chart. Does anybody know of any free software tools for making one of those charts you see on cop shows where they are trying to figure out how a gang is organized (or when they enter the killer's apartment and one wall is this giant conspiracy web thing)?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Explain what?

item.title: Re: update
item.content:encoded: I just had a thought, are the video alterations caused by Hayward in any way related to his hallucinations when he was infected with 1903? They both involve tuxedos/dresses and an animal theme.

item.title: Re: update
item.content:encoded:
Aw, thanks!

And don't worry 'bout it you're not rambling; it's always interesting hearing about people's opinions.

item.title: Re: update
item.content:encoded: I like the new style. It feels like it fits better at the bottom with the logs. This is the 3rd or fourth time I've read this SCP and I immediately noticed the change. What that says about me, I have no idea. Also I've read all your stuff and imo all of it is amazing so far. I've even read the entire short story on your sandbox about when Stuart meets Clovis for the first time, and I can't wait to see where it goes. Anyway, that's enough rambling. Probably didn't need to say all that.

item.title: update
item.content:encoded:
Noting that I toyed with the format a bit. I'll be implementing this style in all of my PH skips individually, if they need it.

This will mostly be used to replace collapsibles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And here we are at the end of things…at least for now. I read the SCP archives from top to bottom. This is a fantastic capstone to the archive (…at least for now!) as well as to the Pitch Haven story, which is easily one of the highlights of this site, with a great scope and mix of suspense, weirdness, adventure, horror, and humanity.

And for my money, Clovis (whom I, too, interpret as the "puppetmaster" of SCP-2999) is one of the best characters and iconic "monsters" to feature on this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Why did I enlarge?

WHY DID I ENLARGE???

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This Might come off as wierd but.. Clovis Kind of reminds me of HABIT from Everyman hybrid.
Clovis and Habit are both related to rabits
they are both possesive spirits, HABIT…with people and Clovis..with computers
they have some unclear goal…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I assumed it was a conlang and never bothered to see if it was just a cipher. I have to say that I find this a little bit disappointing. :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know everything.

More honestly, I'm just good at pattern recognition and research. SCP-2746 is a Rosetta Stone, with extensive side-by-side translations. There are some quirks to the cipher, such as single letters mapping to double letters, but a cursory glance at the language made it clear there were patterns. I realized that the total number of English words matched the total number of language words, and some mapped directly, like the=foy, and I figured out the code from there.

But yeah, if you ever wonder where my brain connects A to Zephyr, that's a window into the process.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Probably from comparison of the "translations" (actually deciphers) to the original documents in SCP-2746.

Although it probably really should be a language and not a cipher, and should definitely not be close to English, as it is the root language that predates even Proto-Indo-European (I think). I think that we can just ignore all that, in favor of it being a crackable Easter egg. In-universe it probably isn't a cipher.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How did you decipher that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a cypher from English, so there's no gender type beyond what's evident in the English.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What language is this? And is there any gender type here so that we can tell who is sorry, a man or a woman?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
LA ÆYI WI' WI'UAUAIL WIOYYFLAY. LA KLAKL'F YIYÆL SI'UA LAF FI' PY FOLAW IOÆIL

Translates to:

I am so sorry sweetie. I didn't mean for it to be this way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems to be the old language found in SCP-2746.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looks like HEX code. Decodes to this:

LA ÆYI WI' WI'UAUAIL WIOYYFLAY. LA KLAKL'F YIYÆL SI'UA LAF FI' PY FOLAW IOÆIL

…which makes it no less gibberish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Isn't Clovis just the cutest little SCP ever ;3.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first image of "Sarah" (actually Clovis) is a jpeg, but if you click to enlarge it links to a gif which flashes an alternate image (that gives you some hints about the backstory).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it me or is that picture a gif?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What the hell is this, there are image flashes on Sarah.jpg, weird image flashes of another image. What kind of sorcery is this?

That s*** made me jump. :(

Though I greatly enjoyed this: http://scp-wiki.wdfiles.com/local--files/scp-2999/2999.gif

item.title: Re: +200!
item.content:encoded: Congratulations, baby!

item.title: Re: +200!
item.content:encoded:
AAAAAAAAA
…
is this real life i never though id ever get an article that high ever aaaaaaaaaaa
Congrats on a second century, Fantem!

item.title: You're most certainly welcome Holaved
item.content:encoded:
I'm very glad this whole thing inspired you to try your hand at writing. It's Always good to hear this sort of thing.

And don't worry, I definitely plan on keeping it up! U^w^U

item.title: Thank you, Fantem.
item.content:encoded:
It's got to be hard to create a solid SCP and tale all at once, let alone an interconnected series of them. I look forward to chasing down your work.

You've even inspired this newbie to finally try to make his first scip idea real.

Keep going!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ㄗitch 卄aven

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just tripped into this 'new canon' through SCP-2988 by way of SCP-2746. Not only do I discover the animals of eden, I am now introduced to the resurrection of the floppy and Patchwork cat? Not even sure where to go from here, except on to read anything else I can find from you.

Is there a canon or hub page for these?

I adore the understated Foundation. Stuart is a loyal agent; his first thought is to contain himself. +1 for one of MANY zingers. Like that picture.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thank you friend!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i read it it's good

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: when the narration of it is finished

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: no safe haven when

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh! I say Clovis is Stuart's mom-daughter because he (along with Sarah, Freddie, and Aggie) made her, but she later on helped give birth to him. (NSH-1898)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Um, arrrhgh…

You are hurting my brain thinking thing with your beautiful wonderful confusing story making. I think I need to have a liedown.

I think…
Spirits that once lived in Eden got incarnated as things and/or people on Earth. Clovis was a rabbit and a member of the scholar caste that worked for Frederick as his spokesbunny or something. I don't get how she is also Stuart's mom, though. I know nothing. I think I need to make a chart (unless someone else has done this).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yups, kinda sorta depends maybe

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Does "me"= Clovis?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So Pawn=Agathos and Paladin=Fredrick?
